title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits · Guided Evidence Sprint
summary: A guided career studio extends dated labor-market evidence brief while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of dated labor-market evidence brief while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.
Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Occupation Vs Title
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The workshop variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Role Hypothesis, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Role Hypothesis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Role Hypothesis, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The workshop variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
